subject: Computing
sub_strand: Generation of Computers and Parts of a Computer and Other Gadgets
content_standard: B4.1.1.1: Identify parts of a computer and technology tools
indicator_description: Identify parts of a computer and technology tools (the mouse, keyboard, monitor, system unit and its components (memory, hard disk drive, CD-ROM etc.), speakers, and peripherals.
examplars: 1. Bring items such as memory(i.e. memory cards, memory sticks), hard disk drive, CD-ROM etc. or pictures of these items to class and ask the learners to label selected items or computer parts in their note books.

subject: Computing
sub_strand: Generation of Computers and Parts of a Computer and Other Gadgets
content_standard: B4.1.1.1: Identify parts of a computer and technology tools
indicator_description: Describe the types of input devices of a computer and their uses. E.g. joystick, light pen, mouse, pointing stick etc. Bring input devices to class.
examplars: 1. Guide learners to explore the types of input devices and their uses.

subject: Computing
sub_strand: Generation of Computers and Parts of a Computer and Other Gadgets
content_standard: B4.1.1.1: Identify parts of a computer and technology tools
indicator_description: Differentiate between RAM and ROM.
examplars:
1. Bring a RAM or ROM to class or a picture of RAM and ROM to class.
2. Guide learners to differentiate between ROM and RAM.

subject: Computing
sub_strand: Generation of Computers and Parts of a Computer and Other Gadgets
content_standard: B4.1.1.1: Identify parts of a computer and technology tools
indicator_description: Type short phrases.
examplars: 1. Guide learners to type different phrases using any Word Processing Application.

subject: Computing
sub_strand: Generation of Computers and Parts of a Computer and Other Gadgets
content_standard: B4.1.1.1: Identify parts of a computer and technology tools
indicator_description: Summarise the first generation of computers which used vacuum tubes as a major piece of technology.
examplars: 1. Guide learners to discuss about the first generation of computers. e.g. ENIAC, EDVAC, UNIVAC, IBM-701 etc.

subject: Computing
sub_strand: Desktop Background and Locations of the Computer.
content_standard: B4.1.2.1: Demonstrate the use of the Desktop Background as well as working with folders.
indicator_description: Describe the desktop background and explain its features (e.g. image, icons and Taskbar of the background).
examplars:
1. Guide learners to explore the desktop background and explain its features (i.e. wallpaper, icons of files and folders you have or may have saved to the desktop, taskbar etc.).
2. Show different desktop background images to learners. Assist them to explain its features.

subject: Computing
sub_strand: Desktop Background and Locations of the Computer.
content_standard: B4.1.2.1: Demonstrate the use of the Desktop Background as well as working with folders.
indicator_description: Demonstrate how to Personalise the Desktop Background using required tools and settings;
examplars:
1. Show projected examples of personalised Desktop Background to learners with the aid of a projector or pictures.
2. Guide learners to personalise the Desktop Background using the required tools and settings.

subject: Computing
sub_strand: Desktop Background and Locations of the Computer.
content_standard: B4.1.2.1: Demonstrate the use of the Desktop Background as well as working with folders.
indicator_description: Demonstrate the use of the Sections of the Taskbar; Start Menu (Location and Launching only), Pinned Applications (Launched / Minimised programmes) and Notification Area or system tray (Date & Time, and Volume).
examplars: 1. Guide learners to explore the use of the Taskbar, Start Menu, and Pinned Applications and Notification Area.

subject: Computing
sub_strand: Desktop Background and Locations of the Computer.
content_standard: B4.1.2.1: Demonstrate the use of the Desktop Background as well as working with folders.
indicator_description: Adding and removing icons from the desktop.
examplars: 1. Guide learners to add and remove icons from the desktop

subject: Computing
sub_strand: Desktop Background and Locations of the Computer.
content_standard: B4.1.2.1: Demonstrate the use of the Desktop Background as well as working with folders.
indicator_description: Moving, selecting and hiding multiple icons on the desktop.
examplars: 1. Guide learners to move, select and hide icons on the desktop.

subject: Computing
sub_strand: Desktop Background and Locations of the Computer.
content_standard: B4.1.2.1: Demonstrate the use of the Desktop Background as well as working with folders.
indicator_description: Creating, naming and renaming a folder.
examplars:
1. Guide learners to create, name and rename a folder.
2. NB: Learners should be guided to play computer games to re-enforce keyboard and mouse skills.

subject: Computing
sub_strand: Data, Sources and Usage
content_standard: B4.1.3.1. Identify Data and collect data from different sources.
indicator_description: Use strategies for identifying data in conversation and newspapers.
examplars:
1. Guide learners to discuss strategies, which can be used to identify data and information.
2. Use different approaches to aid identify data in conversation and newspaper e.g. grouping students, role play etc.

subject: Computing
sub_strand: Data, Sources and Usage
content_standard: B4.1.3.1. Identify Data and collect data from different sources.
indicator_description: Use strategies for classifying data into information (i.e. sorting and classifying)
examplars:
1. Guide learners to identify and record data in the different forms (e.g. age, shoe size, etc.)
2. Assist learners to extract and classify the relevant data into information.

subject: Computing
sub_strand: Data, Sources and Usage
content_standard: B4.1.3.1. Identify Data and collect data from different sources.
indicator_description: Identify primary sources of information (photographs, audio recordings, video recordings, film, letters and diaries, speeches, published books, newspapers and magazines, government publications, oral histories, etc.)
examplars: 1. Guide learners in groups to discuss where one can get data and information and make a presentation on it to the class.

subject: Computing
sub_strand: Data, Sources and Usage
content_standard: B4.1.3.1. Identify Data and collect data from different sources.
indicator_description: Demonstrate Sending and sharing information to and from other gadgets. e.g., radio, fax, telephones calls, SMS etc.
examplars: 1. Guide learners to mention or talk about where one can receive or share information in the community and the world as a whole.

subject: Computing
sub_strand: Data, Sources and Usage
content_standard: B4.1.3.1. Identify Data and collect data from different sources.
indicator_description: Demonstrate basic calculations on sample data e.g. sum, percentage, average etc.
examplars: 1. Guide learners to perform basic calculations on sample data they have been made to collect using sum, average, percentages etc.

subject: Computing
sub_strand: Data, Sources and Usage
content_standard: B4.1.3.2. Demonstrate data collection using Data collection tools.
indicator_description: Identify the steps and issues involved in data collection.
examplars:
1. Guide learners to explore the steps involved: identify reasons, set goals, plan approach, collect and interpret.
2. Guide learners to explore issues involved: unavailability of data, intentional manipulation, random errors.

subject: Computing
sub_strand: Data, Sources and Usage
content_standard: B4.1.3.2. Demonstrate data collection using Data collection tools.
indicator_description: Describe various tools or techniques of data collection e.g. interview, observation, checklist etc.
examplars: 1. Guide learners to explore the tools for collecting data and aid them to make practical examples of the various tools.

subject: Computing
sub_strand: Data, Sources and Usage
content_standard: B4.1.3.2. Demonstrate data collection using Data collection tools.
indicator_description: Draw or sketch maps of the area to be surveyed.
examplars:
1. Bring diagrams or pictures of area(s) surveyed.
2. Guide learners to sketch maps of an area to be surveyed by them.

subject: Computing
sub_strand: Technology in the Community (Communication)
content_standard: B4.1.4.1. Demonstrate the use of technology in the community
indicator_description: Describe how digital systems are used and could be used at home, in school and the local community.
examplars: 1. Guide learners to mention and describe how digital systems (computers, mobile phones, megaphones etc.) are used and could be used at home, in school and the local community.

subject: Computing
sub_strand: Technology in the Community (Communication)
content_standard: B4.1.4.1. Demonstrate the use of technology in the community
indicator_description: Identify and list common examples of energy-efficient gadgets and techniques used in homes, in schools and in the local communities.
examplars:
1. Bring pictures of energy-efficient gadgets or devices to class e.g. energy saving light bulbs, energy-efficient cooking stoves, toilet water saver, solar chargers etc.
2. Guide learners to discuss in groups and present examples of energy-efficient devices and techniques.

subject: Computing
sub_strand: Technology in the Community (Communication)
content_standard: B4.1.4.1. Demonstrate the use of technology in the community
indicator_description: Describe how a range of digital systems (hardware and software) and their peripheral devices can be used for different purposes in the community.
examplars:
1. Guide learners to discuss in groups of three to five, the range of digital systems and their peripheral devices.
2. How they can be used for different purposes in the community. Let learners present their findings to the class.

subject: Computing
sub_strand: Technology in the Community (Communication)
content_standard: B4.1.4.1. Demonstrate the use of technology in the community
indicator_description: Identify technological changes in the community (e.g. Using automated irrigation for farming, eco-friendly pesticides and manure, etc.)
examplars:
1. Guide learners to explore technological changes in the community or in the world.
2. Let learners find examples of technological changes in the community or world around them and present findings.

subject: Computing
sub_strand: Introduction to MS-PowerPoint (Tabs and Ribbons of MS-PowerPoint)
content_standard: B4.2.1.1. Demonstrate the use of MS- PowerPoint
indicator_description: Demonstrate the use of clipboard, slides, fonts, paragraph and editing in the ribbons under the home ribbons section.
examplars: 1. Guide learners to explore the use of the clipboard, slides, fonts, paragraph, and editing features in MS- PowerPoint under the Home tab.

subject: Computing
sub_strand: Introduction to MS-PowerPoint (Tabs and Ribbons of MS-PowerPoint)
content_standard: B4.2.1.1. Demonstrate the use of MS- PowerPoint
indicator_description: Show a 3-slide presentation using clipboard, slides, fonts, paragraph and editing of the ribbons studied.
examplars: 1. Guide learners to prepare and present a prepared project or exercise using clipboard, slides, fonts, paragraph and editing of the ribbons studied their work in MS-PowerPoint to the class.

subject: Computing
sub_strand: Introduction to Word Processing (Tabs and Ribbons of Word Processing)
content_standard: B4.3.1.1. Demonstrate understanding of the use of word processing application
indicator_description: Demonstrate how to use the clipboard, styles, fonts, paragraph and editing.
examplars: 1. Guide learners to use clipboard, styles, fonts, paragraph and editing in the Home Tab of MS-Word.

subject: Computing
sub_strand: Introduction to Word Processing (Tabs and Ribbons of Word Processing)
content_standard: B4.3.1.1. Demonstrate understanding of the use of word processing application
indicator_description: Illustrate the use of the clip board, styles, fonts, paragraph and editing.
examplars: 1. Guide learners to use the clipboard, styles, fonts, paragraph and editing feature under the Home tab and let learners explore on a simple word document.
